codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the requested persistence-after-reload Playwright coverage and adds explicit cutover artifacts (parity matrix + transition timeline). Required-path legacy Bun e2e references are absent from package/workflow/services required paths. I found evidence-quality gaps in the new artifacts, but they do not block functional acceptance of the feature.

issues.description: Transition step 3 records commit as "working-tree" instead of an immutable SHA, which weakens auditability for the ordering claim (VAL-CROSS-003 evidence is less directly traceable to git history).
The artifact uses a fixed generatedAt timestamp rather than run-derived metadata, reducing confidence that the timeline evidence was produced from the reviewed revision.

The matrix marks "Builtin Renderer Fallback" as equivalent_or_stronger via the artifact rendering UI case, but the mapped Playwright test does not assert renderer fallback semantics directly (it asserts visible tool card content; see tests/playwright/artifact-approval.e2e.ts:126-144).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/shared guidance does not currently define a convention for evidence artifacts to use immutable commit references and generated-at metadata derived from execution context. This likely contributed to "working-tree" and fixed timestamp values in committed transition evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: tests/artifacts/playwright/legacy-cutover-transition-timeline.json:4,37; mission AGENTS.md testing guidance describes required evidence types but not metadata conventions for evidence artifacts.

summary: Pass. The feature is implemented and behavior goals are covered (including reload persistence flow and cutover evidence artifacts), with only non-blocking evidence-fidelity gaps in artifact metadata/parity claim precision.
